strategy_summary: The teeth of vampire bats cut into flesh painlessly because they are razor-sharp. “It sinks its razor-sharp teeth into the skin, scoops out a sliver of flesh, and inserts its tongue into the wound…The bite causes little pain, and the animal rarely awakens.” (Crump 2005:68)
mechanism:
The teeth of vampire bats cut into flesh painlessly because they are razor-sharp.

“It sinks its razor-sharp teeth into the skin, scoops out a sliver of flesh, and inserts its tongue into the wound…The bite causes little pain, and the animal rarely awakens.” (Crump 2005:68)
